ddls:
CREATE TABLE "iterations" (
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each iteration */,
  "iteration_type" TEXT /* Type of iteration (e.g., machine operation, process iteration) */,
  "best_frequency" INTEGER /* Best frequency achieved during the iteration */,
  "worst_frequency" INTEGER /* Worst frequency achieved during the iteration */,
  "frequency_average" REAL /* Average frequency calculated for the iteration */,
  "event_average" REAL /* Average number of events occurred during the iteration */,
  "time_spent" REAL /* Time spent in seconds for the iteration */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the iteration record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the iteration record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("iteration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machines" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "machine_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the machine within the facility */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the machine */,
  "machine_category" TEXT /* Category of the machine (e.g., production, testing, maintenance) */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* Maintenance schedule for the machine */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the machine record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the machine record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "processes" (
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each process */,
  "process_name" TEXT /* Name of the process */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the process */,
  "process_category" TEXT /* Category of the process (e.g., production, quality control, packaging) */,
  "process_owner" INTEGER /* ID of the user responsible for the process */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the process record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the process record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("process_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_processes_process_owner FOREIGN KEY ("process_owner") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance data entry */,
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the iteration */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the machine involved */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the process involved */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of performance data (e.g., frequency, event count, time metrics) */,
  "data_value" REAL /* Actual performance data value */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the performance data type */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the performance data was recorded */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the performance data record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the performance data record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_iteration_id FOREIGN KEY ("iteration_id") REFERENCES iterations ("iteration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("process_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, operator, manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the performance data that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "alert_category" TEXT /* Category of the alert (e.g., machine failure, process anomaly, maintenance required) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES performance_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert history record */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the alert */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on how the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the resolution (e.g., resolved, pending, in_progress) */,
  "resolution_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert history record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert history record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_history_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_history_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)
